intro: Uragoga ampla, also known as the "Uragoga," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga ampla include alkaloids, lignans, and flavonoids, such as anonaine, uliginosine, and quercetin, which contribute to its pharmacological effects. In herbal medicine, Uragoga ampla is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
